results.title: Paddington goes to the movies
results.creator_credit: Barry Leith, Graham Clutterbuck, FilmFair Communications and Paddington
results.brief_description: Paddington is caught in a storm and shelters in the entrance of a cinema where he discovers that “Singing in the Rain” starring Gene Kelly is screening. Being an unaccompanied bear under 16, Paddington is not allowed in. However, he manages to peer through a keyhole and catches his first glimpse of the world of the silver screen. Unfortunately, in typical Paddington fashion, he also wreaks havoc in the theatre and the projection box. On his way home, in the excitement of the moment, Paddington tries to emulate the dancing of Gene Kelly. Narrated by Michael Hordern and based on the book by Michael Bond. Suitable for lower and middle primary levels.

results.title: Yvonne's perfume = Le Parfum d'Yvonne [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: Patrice Leconte, Thierry Ganay, Centre European Cinematographique Rhones-Alpes, Lambart Productions, M.G. Films and Zoulou Films
results.brief_description: 1958, a resort hotel on Lake Geneva is the setting for this foray into romance, friendship and decadence. It is at the hotel that Victor meets Dr. Meinthe, a flamboyant homosexual, and Yvonne, a beautiful woman who captures them both. Together, the three of them embark on a summer experience like no other. Cast includes Jean-Pierre Marielle, Hippolyte Girardot and Sandra Majani. In French with English subtitles.

results.title: Le Colonel Chabert
results.creator_credit: Yves Angelo, Jean-Louis Livi, DD Productions and Film Par Film
results.brief_description: Colonel Chabert (Gerard Depardieu) is a decorated soldier who miraculously survives the brutal 1807 Battle of Eylau. So badly injured, it takes the Colonel 10 years to recover and make his way back to Paris. When he returns, he finds himself officially listed as dead. Meanwhile his “widow” (Fanny Ardant) has claimed his fortune and taken a new husband, the politically ambitious Count Ferraud (Andre Dussolier) with whom she has two children. Content with her marriage and social standing, Countess Ferraud refuses to acknowledge the now downtrodden Chabert as her former husband. To regain his name, his fortune, and his wife, Chabert hires the brilliant and ruthless attorney Derville (Fabrice Luchini), while Countess Chabert sets in motion a plan to have him removed from her life once and for all. Adapted from a novel by Honore de Balzac. In French with English sub-titles.

results.title: Maple syrup and sugar
results.brief_description: Explains how maple trees create maple sugar; how it is collected; and how it is then processed into final products. Includes Canadian custom of ‘sugaring off’ - a social event and celebration of spring and harvest time.

results.title: Maple sugar
results.brief_description: Quebec produces about eighty percent of the worlds maple products. The film reports from the heart of the sugar country in Quebec’s Eastern Townships. All stages of processing are shown, from collecting the sap from the trees, to boiling, bottling and marketing. Also includes the Canadian custom of ‘sugaring off’, a celebration of spring and the first maple syrup gathered. Fresh hot syrup is poured onto snow to create maple toffee which is then eaten by everyone.

results.title: Our neighbour: Australia
results.creator_credit: Agha Bashir Ahmad, Rashid Khan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: A general survey of Australia, its resources and its work under the Colombo Plan, as seen through the eyes of a student from South-East Asia. This film is the work of two Pakistan students, who came to Australia under the Colombo Plan to study the profession of film-making at the studios of the Films Division of the Department of the Interior.

results.title: We the jury
results.creator_credit: Jo Lane, Debra Annear and Triptych Films
results.brief_description: This program clearly defines the procedures involved in selecting a jury to serve in a criminal trial. The Victorian example is used to explain the various steps of jury service including: jury selection, how jurors may be challenged, how jurors may be excused form serving and swearing in a jury. Interviews with jurors, a judge and other legal personnel further explain the reasons behind the procedures, providing an insight into the role of the jury. Suitable for middle Secondary - Tertiary level students.
